betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Breeding Reactor§f

§aHarness neutron flux to transmute lighter elements into heavier, more valuable ones!§f

The §bBreeding Reactor§f is a machine that transmutes isotopes into heavier forms using the neutron flux of an adjacent research reactor. When operating, green-ish lightning arcs from the center rod to the outer rods visible through the glass observation window.

§6Operation§f:
• Place rods in the left slot of the GUI
• Adjacent research reactor must provide sufficient flux
• Each recipe requires a minimum flux level
• Sets of rods require more flux than single rods
• Quad rods require only §e3×§f the single rod's flux (not 4×), making them more efficient

§6Breeding Recipes§f:
§b• Thorium-232§f — §e500§f flux → Thorium Fuel | Quad: §e1,500§f
§b• Lithium§f — §e200§f flux → Tritium | Quad: §e600§f
§b• Cobalt§f — §e100§f flux → Cobalt-60 | Quad: §e300§f
§b• Radium-226§f — §e300§f flux → Actinium-227 | Quad: §e900§f
§b• Natural Uranium§f — §e200§f flux → Reactor-Grade Pu | Quad: §e600§f
§b• U-238§f — §e300§f flux → Reactor-Grade Pu | Quad: §e900§f
§b• Reactor-Grade Pu§f — §e200§f flux → Nuclear Waste | Quad: §e600§f
§b• U-235§f — §e300§f flux → Np-237 | Quad: §e900§f
§b• Np-237§f — §e200§f flux → Pu-238 | Quad: §e600§f
§b• Pu-238§f — §e1,000§f flux → Pu-239 | Quad: §e3,000§f

§6Multi-Stage Breeding§f: Some elements chain — U-235 → Np-237 → Pu-238 → Pu-239, each requiring different flux levels.

§eTip§f: This machine is not a reactor on its own — it's a neutron flux harvester. Always pair it with at least one research reactor!
